what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note authored by Tim in the dragnet-development channel; it comments on board item-11e84d01 and clarifies that the term "channels" is meant to describe one system with different capabilities, not three separate systems.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares type=note, source=claude_code, state=posted, author_session=tim, channel=dragnet-development
A links entry of kind commented_on points to board://item-11e84d01
The body is titled with [heading] re: a quote about three different things called "channels"
The note states it is "not meant to be three different systems, meant to be one system with different capabilities and stuff"
The author explicitly says they do not know the code and that describing code would be pointless
No code symbols are present in the file
standouts: It is a design-intent clarification about the meaning of "channels"
It explicitly rejects the interpretation that there are three separate channel systems
It is framed as a comment on an earlier noticeboard item
conventions: Follows the noticeboard markdown format with YAML frontmatter and a Markdown body
Uses board:// URI addressing and a commented_on link kind
Uses ISO 8601 timestamps with timezone offsets
Uses a [heading] marker in the note body
concerns: The clarification is conversational and lacks concrete details about what the capabilities are or how the single system is structured
notes: A posted design note by Tim clarifying that "channels" should be understood as one system with varied capabilities rather than three separate systems, commenting on item-11e84d01.
questions: What are the three different things referred to as "channels"?
What is the content of item-11e84d01 that this note comments on?
What specific capabilities are intended to be part of the single channels system?
How does this design intent map to the actual code or architecture?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to clarify design intent, and the content does exactly that.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to correct a misunderstanding that there are three distinct channel systems and to assert that there is instead one unified channels concept with multiple capabilities.
novelty: It frames a design clarification from a product/intent perspective while explicitly excluding code-level details.
contribution: This file contributes a recorded statement of design intent that "channels" are one system with multiple capabilities, plus a link to the prior item it responds to, preserving conversational design rationale for later fusion.
summary_for_embedding: A posted noticeboard note by Tim in the dragnet-development channel commenting on item-11e84d01, stating that the term "channels" refers to one system with different capabilities rather than three separate systems.
intention_for_embedding: To correct the perception that there are three distinct channel systems and to assert a unified, multi-capability channels concept.
